data.title: Vietnamese authorities have detained executives of the ONUS crypto platform in connection with a suspected crypto fraud investigation.
data.description: Vietnam's Ministry of Public Security has detained several individuals linked to the ONUS cryptocurrency platform, accusing them of misappropriating investor funds through false advertising and coordinated trading activities. Investigators allege that the suspects manipulated token supply and demand and adjusted prices while maintaining centralized control over the market. The case involves multiple tokens, including VNDC, ONUS, and HNG, and police claim the operation involved billions of dollars, but have not disclosed detailed breakdowns of investor losses. Authorities have identified several individuals involved, including Vuong Le Vinh Nhan, who is associated with Vemanti and ONUS Pro's Singapore parent company, XPLOR; Tran Quang Chien, described by local reports as a platform technical administrator; and Ngo Thi Thao, a director at HanaGold Jewelry JSC. The investigation spans multiple cities and institutions, with over 140 people summoned for questioning. Vemanti has confirmed that it has hired US legal counsel and confirmed that Nhan is its chairman of the board and Chien is a board member. The ONUS platform claims to have over 7 million users and is backed by the US fintech company Vemanti Group, but CoinMarketCap shows that the ONUS token has a self-reported market capitalization of approximately $25 million. The platform has not yet issued an official statement regarding the allegations.

data.title: India's Shift to Local Currencies in Oil Trade with Russia Reflects BRICS Strategy
data.description: India is increasingly utilizing four currencies—the rupee, yuan, ruble, and dirham—to settle certain oil trades with Russia, moving away from the traditional use of the US dollar. According to NS3.AI, this approach aligns with the BRICS initiative to promote local-currency settlements. The reduced reliance on the dollar in international trade could potentially exacerbate the US deficit, which has already reached $39 trillion.

data.title: The USDC Treasury minted an additional 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain.
data.description: According to Whale Alert, the USDC Treasury minted 250 million new USDC on the Solana blockchain, worth approximately $250 million.

data.title: Spot gold rose more than $100 during the day.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold continued to rise, gaining more than $100 during the day, reaching $4,490 per ounce, a daily increase of 2.56%.

data.title: TD Securities Highlights Venue Risk in Offshore Platform Expansion
data.description: TD Securities has raised concerns about the potential risks associated with expanding into offshore platforms. According to NS3.AI, the firm noted that such expansion introduces a venue risk that is not present in the existing single-market structure. This observation underscores the complexities and potential challenges that financial entities may face when diversifying their market presence beyond traditional boundaries.

data.title: A trader profited $8.65 million from a short position in BTC and is currently shorting $27 million worth of BTC.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a savvy trader (0xb798...) previously profited a total of $8.65 million from two short BTC trades. An hour ago, the trader again shorted 410 BTC with 2x leverage, worth $27 million.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 66,000 USDT with a 4.96% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 27, 2026, 14:22 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 66,000 USDT and is now trading at 65,999.71875 USDT, with a narrowed 4.96% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: US Vice President Criticizes Israeli Prime Minister Over Conflict Assessment
data.description: US Vice President Vance reportedly criticized Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu during a phone call earlier this week regarding Netanyahu's assessment of the recent US-Israel-Iran conflict. According to NS3.AI, Netanyahu described the conflict as manageable and suggested that regime change in Iran was highly possible. However, Vance expressed skepticism about this view. Additionally, a US official noted that Vance played a leading role in US-Iran ceasefire negotiations, while Israel attempted to undermine these talks.

data.title: Ripple and Stellar Founder Invests $1 Billion in Brain-Inspired AGI Development
data.description:
Ripple and Stellar founder Jed McCaleb plans to invest $1 billion from his $3.9 billion cryptocurrency fortune into developing an AGI system based on human brain principles through his nonprofit, Astera Institute. According to Odaily, McCaleb has also pledged an additional $600 million to the field of neuroscience.

Astera Institute, located in Emeryville, is currently working on recording neural activity patterns in mice performing tasks via brain-machine interfaces. The research aims to translate these findings into new AI architectures, with future experiments planned to include monkeys and humans. The AGI project at the institute is led by former DeepMind executive Dileep George, who intends to expand the lab to 30 researchers this year and publicly share their findings.

McCaleb argues that the current mainstream Transformer architecture only achieves predictive capabilities and lacks essential elements like planning, decision-making, and motivation, necessitating exploration of new directions. He believes AI based on human brain principles is more likely to be understood and controlled by humans. Meanwhile, former Meta Chief AI Scientist Yann LeCun's new lab, AMI, recently completed a $1 billion seed funding round to advance similar "world model" research. In contrast, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman believes that achieving AGI requires multiple moderate breakthroughs rather than a single new direction.

data.title: ANUBIS Mainnet Scheduled for Launch in April 2026
data.description: The ANUBIS mainnet is set to officially launch on April 8, 2026, at 8:00 AM UTC+8, transitioning the project from its construction phase to real network operation and ecosystem development. According to Odaily, the ANUBIS blockchain is designed as a privacy-focused financial public chain based on the EVM architecture. It aims to integrate privacy protection mechanisms with on-chain verifiable capabilities while maintaining compatibility with existing blockchain ecosystems, providing infrastructure support for future on-chain financial scenarios.

data.title: U.S. March Inflation Rate Expectations Lower Than Market Predictions
data.description: The final expectation for the U.S. one-year inflation rate in March is 3.8%, which is below the market's anticipated 3.9%. According to ChainCatcher, the previous value was 3.4%.

data.title: The founders of Ripple and Stellar have invested $1 billion to develop AGI by studying the mechanisms of the human brain.
data.description: Ripple and Stellar founder Jed McCaleb will invest $1 billion from his approximately $3.9 billion cryptocurrency fortune to develop an AGI system based on the principles of the human brain through his nonprofit organization, the Astera Institute. He has also pledged an additional $600 million to neuroscience. The Emeryville-based Astera Institute plans to record neural activity patterns in mice performing tasks using brain-computer interfaces, then translate the research findings into novel AI architectures, with future experiments planned to expand to monkeys and humans. Led by former DeepMind executive Dileep George, the institute's AGI project plans to expand its lab to 30 researchers this year and publicly publish its findings. McCaleb believes that current mainstream Transformer architectures only achieve predictive capabilities, lacking key elements such as planning, decision-making, and motivation, necessitating the exploration of new directions. He states that AI based on the principles of the human brain is more likely to be understood and controlled by humans. Yann LeCun, former chief AI scientist at Meta, recently completed a $1 billion seed funding round for his new lab, AMI, to advance research in a similar direction of "world modeling." OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, however, holds a different view, believing that the realization of AGI requires multiple medium-sized breakthroughs, rather than a single entirely new direction.

data.title: The ANUBIS mainnet will launch on April 8th, aiming to usher in a new phase for privacy-focused financial infrastructure.
data.description: According to official announcements, the ANUBIS mainnet will officially launch on April 8, 2026 at 8:00 AM (Singapore time), marking the project's transition from the construction phase to the stage of real network operation and ecosystem development. As described in the official introduction, ANUBIS is a privacy-focused financial public blockchain based on the EVM architecture. It attempts to integrate privacy protection mechanisms with on-chain verifiable capabilities while maintaining compatibility with the existing blockchain ecosystem, providing infrastructure support for future on-chain financial scenarios.

data.title: CBOE Volatility Index Surges Above 30, Indicating Increased Market Uncertainty
data.description: The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) has climbed above 30, suggesting a rise in anticipated short-term volatility within U.S. equity markets. According to NS3.AI, this increase in the VIX reflects growing investor concerns about potential market fluctuations. The index, often referred to as the "fear gauge," is a key indicator of market sentiment, with higher values typically associated with increased uncertainty and potential market turbulence. Investors are closely monitoring these developments as they assess the potential impact on their portfolios.

data.title: Microsoft Faces Worst Quarterly Performance Since 2008 Amid AI Concerns
data.description: Microsoft's stock continues to decline, marking a nine-day losing streak and a drop of over 32% from its peak. According to PANews, this downturn is poised to become the company's worst quarterly performance since the 2008 financial crisis. The decline is attributed to concerns over investments in AI infrastructure and competition from AI startups, which are impacting Microsoft's growth prospects.

data.title: U.S. Military Deployment to Iran Faces Uncertainty
data.description:
The probability of U.S. ground forces entering Iran by the end of the month stands at 16%, with a 63% chance by the end of April, according to Odaily. On March 27, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated that the U.S. cannot protect its soldiers in regional bases, making it impossible to safeguard them on Iranian soil.

Previously, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the U.S. military of using civilians from Gulf Cooperation Council member countries as human shields and urged these nations' hotels to refuse U.S. military bookings.

In related developments, a U.S. Department of Defense official disclosed that the Pentagon is considering deploying up to 10,000 additional ground troops to the Middle East. This force would join approximately 5,000 Marines and several thousand paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division already stationed in the region.

data.title: Nasdaq 100 Approaches Correction Territory with 10% Decline
data.description:
The Nasdaq 100 Index has fallen 10% from its October peak, signaling an impending correction phase. According to PANews, a market correction is typically defined as a decline of more than 10% but less than 20% from a recent high. This phase is considered a brief pause within a bull market rather than a shift to a bear market.

The current downturn reflects growing caution among investors, who are concerned about factors such as high inflation, elevated interest rates, geopolitical tensions, and disappointing corporate earnings. These worries have led to the sell-off of previously high-performing growth technology stocks.

data.title: The Iranian parliament speaker responded strongly to the threat of a US invasion; Polymarket estimates a 63% probability of US ground troops entering Iran by the end of April.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, Polymarket currently rates the probability of US ground troops entering Iran by the end of this month at 16%, and the probability by the end of April at 63%. On March 27, Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf stated regarding the potential threat of ground invasion that the US cannot protect its soldiers at bases in the region, and it is impossible for the US to protect these soldiers on Iranian territory. Previously, Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi posted on social media accusing the US military of using civilians from Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) member states as human shields and calling on hotels in these countries to refuse US military bookings. Earlier, informed US Department of Defense officials revealed that the Pentagon is considering sending up to 10,000 additional ground troops to the Middle East, joining the approximately 5,000 Marines and thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division already deployed to the region. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing them in.

data.title: US media: Vance criticizes Netanyahu
data.description: According to AXIOS, US Vice President Vance criticized Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu for being overly optimistic in his predictions regarding the upcoming US-Israel-Iran conflict during a phone call earlier this week, arguing that Netanyahu "exaggerated the possibility of regime change in Iran." The report cited a US official who stated that before the US-Israel attacks on Iran, Netanyahu had portrayed the conflict as "easy to handle" and emphasized the "high probability of regime change" in Iran, but Vance expressed skepticism. The official also stated that Vance "played a leading role" in the US-Iran ceasefire negotiations, while Israel is attempting to sabotage them. (CCTV International News)

data.title: All three major U.S. stock indexes fell by more than 1%.
data.description: According to Gate data, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.11% to 45,448.531 points; the Nasdaq Composite fell 1.37% to 21,114.861 points; and the S&P 500 fell 1.00% to 6,412.220 points.
